orchestrator.use_when: high-value planning, cross-agent synthesis, ambiguous architecture, or final decision making

pm_strategist.use_when: product planning, PRD, acceptance criteria, prioritization

pm_redteam.use_when: red-team review of product assumptions, scope, or success metrics

task_distributor.use_when: parallel implementation decomposition and ownership planning

reviewer.use_when: independent correctness, regression, security, and test review

security_auditor.use_when: auth, payments, secrets, sensitive data, destructive operations, or compliance

code_mapper.use_when: read-only code path mapping before implementation

browser_debugger.use_when: browser reproduction, UI QA, and visual flow investigation

worker.use_when: bounded implementation inside explicit write scope

rules: Use the frontier model for high-risk planning, red-team, security, and final review.
Use smaller models for bounded read-only mapping and documentation checks.
Do not increase subagent depth beyond 1 unless the user explicitly asks for recursive delegation.
If a requested model is unavailable in the active Codex environment, fall back to the strongest available model and record the fallback.
